title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
desc: The story of Victor Frankenstein and the creature he brings to life — a landmark of Gothic and science fiction.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
desc: Elizabeth Bennet navigates issues of manners, upbringing, morality, education, and marriage in Georgian England.

title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
author: Lewis Carroll
desc: Alice falls through a rabbit hole into a fantasy world populated by peculiar creatures.

title: A Modest Proposal
author: Jonathan Swift
desc: A satirical essay suggesting the poor of Ireland ease their economic troubles by selling their children as food.

title: Moby Dick
author: Herman Melville
desc: The obsessive quest of Captain Ahab for revenge against Moby Dick, the giant white sperm whale.

title: A Tale of Two Cities
author: Charles Dickens
desc: Set in London and Paris before and during the French Revolution, a story of love, sacrifice and resurrection.

title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Arthur Conan Doyle
desc: Twelve stories featuring the brilliant detective Sherlock Holmes and his loyal companion Dr. Watson.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
desc: A young man sells his soul for eternal youth and beauty while his portrait ages and reflects his moral corruption.

title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
author: Mark Twain
desc: Huck Finn and runaway slave Jim travel down the Mississippi River on a raft in search of freedom.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
desc: Count Dracula attempts to move from Transylvania to England to find new blood, opposed by Professor Van Helsing.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
desc: Orphan Pip's journey from poverty to wealth, and his encounters with the mysterious Miss Havisham.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
desc: A young student plans and executes a murder, then struggles with guilt, paranoia and redemption.

title: The Yellow Wallpaper
author: Charlotte Perkins Gilman
desc: A woman's descent into madness as she becomes obsessed with the yellow wallpaper in her bedroom.

title: Ulysses
author: James Joyce
desc: A day in the life of Leopold Bloom in Dublin — one of the most celebrated novels in the English language.

title: Heart of Darkness
author: Joseph Conrad
desc: A voyage up the Congo River into the heart of Africa, exploring colonialism and the darkness of human nature.

title: Metamorphosis
author: Franz Kafka
desc: Gregor Samsa wakes one morning to find himself transformed into a giant insect.

title: Emma
author: Jane Austen
desc: Emma Woodhouse, handsome, clever and rich, with a comfortable home and happy disposition.

title: The Prince
author: Niccolò Machiavelli
desc: A 16th-century political treatise on how to acquire and maintain political power.

title: Beowulf
author: Anonymous
desc: The oldest surviving long poem in Old English — the hero Beowulf battles the monster Grendel.

title: Little Women
author: Louisa May Alcott
desc: The coming-of-age story of the four March sisters — Meg, Jo, Beth and Amy — during the American Civil War.
